element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns supply-and-demand economy with minimal opposition
Core building material introduced before military needs arise
Population sustenance mechanics learned early to prevent collapse
Economic complexity increases; players manage production chains
Reconnaissance introduced before combat, lower risk engagement
Defensive strategy precedes aggressive military action
Combat encounters increase in frequency and complexity
Expansion strategy becomes primary late-game objective
Players manage economy and military against multiple factions (Nubians, Vikings, Japanese)
Portal mechanics unlock new maps/scenarios; story gates progression

typical_relief_triggers: Military threat defeated or pushed back
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension through real-time economy management; combat spikes interrupt peaceful building phases; later missions intensify frequency of military threats

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Free Game mode allows custom map selection and difficulty tweaking; players can avoid high-difficulty scenarios

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction: single opponent, abundant resources, focus on settlement building and economy stabilization
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate increase: introduction of military mechanics, defensive structures required, first combat encounters, competing for territory
difficulty_curve.late_game: Steep increase: multiple enemy factions simultaneously, territorial conquest objectives, complex supply chains under pressure, heavy combat involvement
notable_spikes: First military encounter (transition from building to defense)
Mission CD campaign (increased difficulty compared to original campaign)

notable_level_patterns.description: Early missions establish strong economic systems (resource production, storage, worker assignment) before introducing combat pressure
Military elements introduced progressively: scouts first (reconnaissance, low risk), then stationary defenses (towers/walls, passive), then offensive units
Magical portals unlock new islands and scenarios; story of Romans shipwrecked trying to return home drives mission sequencing
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Campaign story missions across all chapters

notable_level_patterns.description: Four aesthetically distinct races (Romans, Nubians, Vikings, Japanese) available for competitive Free Game scenarios; balance achieved through gameplay mechanics rather than stats
Later missions require gaining possession of specific territory sections; combined defense and offense simultaneously
